content.problemStatement:
Chef was invited to the party of **N** people numbered from **1** to **N**. Chef knows the growth of all the people, i.e. he knows the growth of the **i**th person is denoted by an integer **Ai** not exceeding **M**.

Chef decided to have some fun. At first, he forms **K** groups of people. The **i**th group consists of all the people numbered from **Li** to **Ri**. Groups may overlap too. 



For each group, Chef wants to know the following information: the maximum difference between the numberings of two people having same growth.  Formally, Chef wants to know the following:


**max{|x − y| : Li ≤ x, y ≤ Ri** and **Ax = Ay}**

Please help Chef to have fun.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains three space-separated integers N, M, and K, denoting the number of people, the maximum growth, and the number of groups respectively. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the growth of people. Then the i-th line of the next K lines contains two space-separated integers Li, Ri, denoting the i-th group.
content.outputFormat: For each group, output the integer denoting the maximum difference between the numbering of two people having the same growth in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each group, find the maximum difference between the numbering of two people having the same growth.
For each group, find the maximum difference between the numbering of two people having the same growth.
For each group, find the maximum difference between the numbering of two people having the same growth.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over each group and for each group, we can iterate over the people in that group to find the maximum difference between the numbering of two people having the same growth. We can use a hashmap to store the indices of the people with the same growth to optimize the solution.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N*K) per test case, O(T*N*K) total. Precomputation is not required.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the hashmap to store the indices of the people with the same growth.
